name: Donald Fagen
abstract: Donald Jay Fagen (, FAY-gən; born January 10, 1948) is an American singer-songwriter and musician who is the co-founder, lead singer, co-songwriter, and keyboardist of the rock band Steely Dan, formed in the early 1970s with musical partner Walter Becker. In addition to his contributions to Steely Dan, Fagen has released four solo albums…

concerts.label: Lauri Strauss Leukemia Foundation Benefit Concert
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: His versatility has enabled him to record with a lengthy list of diverse artists including Donald Fagen
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Rob Mounsey
about_quotes.evidence: they called me in to play the piano solo... Donald Fagen said... just play, comp a little bit through the rest of the track
about_quotes.doc_title: Rob Mounsey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 9, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Catherine Russell
about_quotes.evidence: that was the band that turned into the back-up band for Donald Fagen... So in those days you had cassettes in the answering machine. So every time somebody famous called me I would keep the cassette. So you know I have: “Hi Catherine, this is Donald Fagen.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Catherine Russell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 26, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I was just, I just saw uh Donald Fagen. We were both given the ASCAP Wall of Fame, Wall of Fame award.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
